nfcu_sup_brief_dual_incident.ai_message: Two events are compounding into one queue spike. The east-region SIP fiber cut took ~30% of voice capacity offline; the Marketing Cloud rate promo drove auto-loan volume to 3.2x baseline. Combined effect: 214 in queue, 18.4% abandonment, retry-loop feedback as dropped callers redial. Service level on auto loans is at 54% and falling. Live queue snapshot is below.

nfcu_sup_turn_2_recommendations.ai_message:
Based on historical promotion patterns, this volume will sustain for 4–6 hours and taper by 2 PM. I recommend three actions:

**(1) Activate 8 cross-trained agents** from general banking to auto loans — projected to reduce wait time to under 4 minutes within 30 minutes.

**(2) Enable callback option** for members waiting over 5 minutes — historically reduces abandonment by 34%.

**(3) Flag Marketing Cloud** to require 48-hour Contact Center notification for future promotions above 20,000 recipients.

These three actions together recover service level to 83% within 45 minutes based on historical intervention data.

nfcu_sup_no_accept_plan.ai_message:
If none of the 3 agents accept overtime, I have a three-part contingency:

**(1) Expand to the secondary roster.** 4 additional agents hold mortgage servicing certification but have not accepted OT in the last 60 days. I can send them flexible-shift offers — 2-hour or 4-hour slots instead of a full evening. Historical response rate for flexible offers is 64% vs. 34% for full-shift requests.

**(2) Reduce the coverage requirement with routing.** If I enable extended callback mode starting at 2 PM Friday and shift general banking overflow to auto loans agents already rostered, Friday evening mortgage call load drops from an estimated 340 to ~265. That reduces the agent requirement from 12 to 9 — closeable with 1 OT acceptance from the secondary roster.

**(3) Mandatory coverage as last resort.** NFCU policy allows mandatory assignment to the 2 most junior eligible agents on a critical-coverage shift when 3+ agents decline. This guarantees coverage but typically generates PTO requests the following week. Use only if options 1 and 2 both fail.

**Recommendation:** Start with flexible-slot offers to the 4 secondary agents now. Responses typically come in within 2–3 hours, leaving enough time to confirm coverage before end of day.
suggested_chips: Send flexible-slot offers to secondary agents

confidence.rationale: Secondary-coverage scenario has no recent comparable in last 18 months. Flexible-offer 64% rate is from a 4-event sample — too sparse for high confidence. Mandatory-coverage path has policy-compliance variance.

nfcu_sup_caller_profile.ai_message:
Profiling the auto loan callers from this morning's surge:

**Total volume:** 127 currently waiting + 84 already handled (43 via callback, 41 completed)
**Member type:** 68% are existing NFCU auto loan holders — calling about refinancing into the 2.9% promotional rate. 32% are new inquiries with no existing auto loan.
**Geography:** Heavily Pacific Time. The 7:15 AM Eastern email landed at 4:15 AM on the West Coast. Members are calling during their morning commute (11 AM–1 PM Eastern).
**Call themes (AI topic modeling from Dynamics 365):**
• 'Can I refinance my current rate?' — 51%
• 'What are the exact terms and eligibility?' — 29%
• 'How long does the process take?' — 20%

**Why handle time is elevated:** Rate comparison questions require agents to use the loan calculator tool — adding ~2:38 to average handle time vs. standard auto loan calls. These are warm, interested callers — not complaints. They're conversion opportunities.

**Recommendation:** Agents should lead with the rate calculator tool immediately and have the promotional FAQ open. This recovers ~90 seconds of AHT per call.

confidence.rationale: Call-theme percentages from Dynamics 365 topic modeling have 0.91 precision on this volume; member-type split from CRM is authoritative.
